product_scope: itpay is the single public CLI entry point, and $itpay is its user-facing Skill invocation. Under that one product entry point, the two top-level commerce actions are buy and sell: Buyer workflows are available now; Seller workflows will use the same entry point and are not implemented yet.
title: Canonical Cart And Checkout Routing
purpose: Use the canonical server Cart and route service-backed lines through Service Execution without hardcoded service logic.
when_to_use: A catalog item or variant is ready to add.
An interrupted Cart or Checkout needs recovery.

commands.success_signal: result identifies the canonical cart line and next contains one executable recovery or service command
service-backed lines route to services next; ordinary lines route to buy
status is human_checkout_required and handoff contains only fields usable by the current Host

commands.intent: create or resume a service Checkout
commands.success_signal: one Checkout handoff is returned; rerun with --resume after output or token loss

agent_rules: The Backend Cart is business truth; ~/.itpay-v3/cart.json only stores local recovery handles and explicit drafts.
Run the exact next.command returned by the CLI. Do not extract or invent a capability from a raw list.
One service intent uses one Service Execution. Start another execution for another independent intent.
Ask for email only when the selected capability requires protected delivery; never invent contact data.
A lost Checkout response is retried with the same Cart and persisted idempotency operation.
forbidden: Do not call ordinary buy for a service-backed Cart line.
Do not create a second Checkout when the existing one can be resumed.
Do not treat QR display as payment verification.
